---
title: "Cleaning data for occurrence and community databases" "Database #1" 
subtitle: 'EpIG volunteership'
format: html
editor: visual
date: today
author: "Kelly Daniela Zuluaga" 
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```


# Cargar librerias y revisar información

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(sf)
library(CoordinateCleaner)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringi)

spp_link <- read.csv("data/Text/1_Raw/text__CR2024.csv")

head(spp_link)
View(spp_link)
str(spp_link)
glimpse(spp_link)
```

# Revisar cada una de las columnas

```{r}
# Inspect unique values of key categorical fields

unique(spp_link$basisOfRecord)
#only one unique value:"PreservedSpecimen. No changes required"

unique(spp_link$taxonRank)
#only one unique value.. No changes required for now"

unique(spp_link$lifeForm)

unique(spp_link$institutionCode)

unique(spp_link$collectionCode)

unique(spp_link$datasetID)

str(spp_link$eventDate)

summary(spp_link$eventDate)

head(spp_link$eventDate, 20)
```

#Detectar información dañada en las columnas

```{r}
str(db)


str(spp_link$occurrenceID)
str(spp_link$basisOfRecord)
str(spp_link$typeStatus)
str(spp_link$associatedReferences)
str(spp_link$institutionCode)
str(spp_link$taxonRank)
str(spp_link$collectionCode)
str(spp_link$recordedBy)
str(spp_link$recordNumber)
str(spp_link$identifiedBy)
str(spp_link$lifeForm)
str(spp_link$datasetID)
str(spp_link$matchCodeEpig)
str(spp_link$matchedNameEpig)
str(spp_link$decimalLatitude)
str(spp_link$decimalLongitude)
str(spp_link$eventDate)
str(spp_link$changes)

# Show the structure of every column automatically
lapply(spp_link, str)
```

#Corregir letras con signos raros

```{r}
spp_link$stateProvince <- gsub("Amap�", "Amapa", spp_link$stateProvince)

spp_link$stateProvince <- gsub("Cear�", "Ceara", spp_link$stateProvince)

spp_link$stateProvince <- gsub("Esp�rito Santo", "Espirito Santo", spp_link$stateProvince)

spp_link$stateProvince <- gsub("Goi�s", "Goias", spp_link$stateProvince)

spp_link$stateProvince <- gsub("Maranh�o", "Maranhao", spp_link$stateProvince)

spp_link$stateProvince <- gsub("Par�", "Para", spp_link$stateProvince)

spp_link$stateProvince <- gsub("Paran�", "Parana", spp_link$stateProvince)

spp_link$stateProvince <- gsub("Piau�", "Piaui", spp_link$stateProvince)

spp_link$stateProvince <- gsub("Rond�nia", "Rondonia", spp_link$stateProvince)

spp_link$stateProvince <- gsub("S�o Paulo", "Sao Paulo", spp_link$stateProvince)


```

#Completar los espacios vacios

```{r}

# 1.Crear objeto para revisar los espacios vacios en stateProvince 
stateProvince_vacios <- spp_link %>%
  filter(stateProvince == "" | is.na(stateProvince)) %>%
  select(stateProvince, locality, occurrenceID)

stateProvince_vacios

# 2. Completar el stateProvince basado en palabras de locality 

spp_link <- spp_link %>%
  mutate(stateProvince = case_when(
    (stateProvince == "" | is.na(stateProvince)) & grepl("Bom Jardim", locality, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Rio de Janeiro",
    (stateProvince == "" | is.na(stateProvince)) & grepl("Santana do Pirapama", locality, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Minas Gerais",
    (stateProvince == "" | is.na(stateProvince)) & grepl("Mato Grosso", locality, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Mato Grosso",
    (stateProvince == "" | is.na(stateProvince)) & grepl("Fazenda Santa Maria", locality, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Parana",
    TRUE ~ stateProvince
  ))

# 3. Completar el stateProvince basado en occurrenceID

oID_vacios <- c("assis_i_2026_specieslink_00633", "assis_i_2026_specieslink_00636", "assis_i_2026_specieslink_00894", "assis_i_2026_specieslink_01280", "assis_i_2026_specieslink_01540")

spp_link <- spp_link %>%
  mutate(stateProvince = ifelse((stateProvince == "" | is.na(stateProvince)) & occurrenceID %in% oID_vacios,
                                "Rio de Janeiro",
                                stateProvince))
spp_link <- spp_link %>%
  mutate(stateProvince = ifelse(occurrenceID == "assis_i_2026_specieslink_00691" & (stateProvince == "" | is.na(stateProvince)),
                                "Sao Paulo",
                                stateProvince))
```

#Detectar información dañada en "locality"

```{r}
conteo_locality <- spp_link %>%
  count(locality, sort = TRUE)

conteo_locality
```

#This is for creating the columns and indicate that are being corrected.

```{r}
# make sure ok.2 exists
if (!"ok.2" %in% names(spp_link)) spp_link$ok.2 <- spp_link$.ok_for_analysis #Verificación de la existencia del nombre ok.2, si no existe que se cree

# make sure discardReason exists
if (!"discardReason" %in% names(spp_link)) spp_link$discardReason <- NA_character_

codesplink <- dfsplink_corrected$occurrenceID 
codesplink <- c("cada ocurrenceID","agrega todos los que corregiste")
codesplink

for (code in codesplink) {
  idx <- which(tu_referencia$occurrenceID == code) #La base dea datos con los ocurrendeID que corregiste
  if (length(idx)) {
    spp_link$ok.2[idx] <- TRUE
    spp_link$discardReason[idx] <- "Corrected: points were corrected according to locality"
  }
}
```
